name: openclaw-tavily-search
tagline: ClawHub (266★): Web search via Tavily API (alternative to Brave). Use when the user asks to search the web / look up sources / find links a
verdict: Main risk: the entire flow depends on a live external API call, yet nothing says what to do if the key is missing, the script errors, or the network call fails -- it just breaks with no fallback. The exposed directory name also doesn't match its own skill name, likely a bulk-export artifact.

highlights.summary: The skill's entire flow depends on a working external API call, yet nowhere does it say what to do if the key is missing/invalid, the script errors, or the network/API call fails —
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, no dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
python3 is used in every command but is never listed in the Requirements section or checked for availability (only the API key is called out as a requirement).
